headline: BlackRock Stock Sinks After It Caps Withdrawals From Private Credit Fund
summary: BlackRock shares tumbled after the asset manager limited withdrawals from one of its flagship private credit funds for the first time.  The HPS Corporate Lending Fund, a non-traded business development company marketed to individuals, said it will not repurchase shares beyond the promised 5% liquidity threshold it offers each quarter, after receiving investor requests to redeem more than 5% of total shares for the first time.  The fund has generated a 10.7% annualized return net of fees since its inception four years ago.

raw.headline: BlackRock Stock Sinks After It Caps Withdrawals From Private Credit Fund

The fund has generated a 10.7% annualized return net of fees since its inception four years ago.

headline: BlackRock Expands iBonds ETFs And Launches Leveraged Loan Fund As Shares Lag
summary: BlackRock has announced a broad expansion of its iBonds ETF platform across Treasuries, TIPS, corporates, high yield, and municipal bonds. The company is also launching the iShares Broad USD Floating Rate Loan ETF, its first index based ETF providing access to U.S. leveraged loans. These launches highlight new ways for investors to approach bond laddering and gain index based access to the leveraged loan market. BlackRock, trading on the NYSE under ticker NYSE:BLK, is rolling out these...
